zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary le Leche於大約1295年出生在蘇格蘭艾爾金爾時，她的父親John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk26歲，母親Lucy Cawarden27歲。她於1322年與John Grant 4th of Stratherrick在蘇格蘭音巴內-爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1356年在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安享年62歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mary le Leche于大约1295年出生在苏格兰艾爾金爾时，她的父亲John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk26岁，母亲Lucy Cawarden27岁。她于1322年与John Grant 4th of Stratherrick在苏格兰音巴內-爾结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1356年在苏格兰米德洛迪安享年62岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Leche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: variant of   Letch   or   Leech  .

variants.name: Letch
meanings: English: locative name, either topographic from Middle English lech(e), lecche, lache ‘sluggish stream, wet place’ (Old English * læcc(e), * lecc(e) ‘stream’), or toponymic from a place named with this word, such as Lach Dennis and Lache (both Cheshire) and Eastleach and Northleach (both Gloucs).

variants.name: Leech
meanings: Irish (Galway):English name adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó MaolMhaodhóg (see Logue ).

nameMeanings.name: Mary
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Latin Maria via Old Welsh Meir. See Mary .

en.lifeSummary: When Mary le Leche was born about 1295, in Elginshire, Scotland, her father, John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk, was 26 and her mother, Lucy Cawarden, was 27. She married John Grant 4th of Stratherrick in 1322, in Inverness-shire, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. She died in 1356, in Midlothian, Scotland, at the age of 62, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary le Leche est née environ 1295, à Elginshire, Écosse, son père, John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Lucy Cawarden, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé John Grant 4th of Stratherrick en 1322, à Inverness-shire, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1356, à Midlothian, Écosse, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterrée à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary le Leche um 1295 in Elginshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk 26 und ihre Mutter Lucy Cawarden 27. Sie hat 1322 in Inverness-shire, Scotland John Grant 4th of Stratherrick geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1356 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Midlothian, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary le Leche è nata il circa 1295 nel Elginshire, Scotland, sua padre, John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Lucy Cawarden, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato John Grant 4th of Stratherrick nel 1322, nel Inverness-shire, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1356, nel Midlothian, Scotland, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolta a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Mary le Lecheが約1295年にスコットランドエルジンシュアイアで生まれた時，父John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolkは26歳，母Lucy Cawardenは27歳であった。1322年にスコットランドインバーネス-シュアイアでJohn Grant 4th of Stratherrickと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1356年にスコットランドミドロスアンで62歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mary le Leche(이)가 대략 1295년 스컷랜드 엘긴실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lucy Cawarden(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1322년 스컷랜드 인버네스-실르에서 John Grant 4th of Stratherrick(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1356년 스컷랜드 미들로스이언에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mary le Leche nasceu aproximadamente 1295, em Elginshire, Scotland, seu pai, John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Lucy Cawarden, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com John Grant 4th of Stratherrick em 1322, em Inverness-shire, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1356, em Midlothian, Scotland, com 62 anos, e foi sepultada em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mary le Leche родилась около 1295 г. в местности элгиншир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Lucy Cawarden, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Grant 4th of Stratherrick в 1322 г. в местности инвирнесс-шир, Шотландия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1356 г. в местности мидлотян, Шотландия в возрасте 62 лет и была похоронена в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mary le Leche nació aproximadamente 1295, en Elginshire, Scotland, su papá, John le Leche Esq. of Eggemere And Wrighton, Norfolk, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Lucy Cawarden, tenía 27 años. Se casó con John Grant 4th of Stratherrick en 1322, en Inverness-shire, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió en 1356, en Midlothian, Scotland, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultada en Scotland.
